verses.3: My mouth will speak words of wisdom.
verses.4: I will incline my ear to a proverb.
verses.5: Why should I fear in the days of evil,
verses.6: Those who trust in their wealth,
verses.7: none of them can by any means redeem his brother,
verses.8: For the redemption of their life is costly,
verses.9: that he should live on forever,
verses.10: For he sees that wise men die;
verses.11: Their inward thought is that their houses will endure forever,
verses.12: But man, despite his riches, doesn’t endure.
verses.13: This is the destiny of those who are foolish,
verses.14: They are appointed as a flock for Sheol.
verses.15: But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol,
verses.16: Don’t be afraid when a man is made rich,
verses.17: for when he dies he will carry nothing away.
verses.18: Though while he lived he blessed his soul—
verses.19: he shall go to the generation of his fathers.
verses.20: A man who has riches without understanding,
